core_mechanism: Noise-driven metastable switching in a bistable (double-well) potential where a weakening linear restoring/mean-reverting force lengthens residence times in each of two attractor states, shifting the process from short-memory exponential relaxation toward long-memory persistence
brief_reason: The abstract reports increasing bimodality with two separated states, longer residence times in each regime, weaker short-run correction of shocks, fewer reversals, and traces persisting longer than short-memory exponential decay predicts, which is exactly a bistable stochastic system with a weakening restoring force and slow metastable switching.
